observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the employees concern not allowed to be assigned a motor vehicle/motorcycle and the official with more than one motor vehicle issuance to return or surrender to the Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate for the cancellation of the Property Acknowledgement Report (PAR) and immediate issuance to other Department Heads entitled to service vehicle or be pooled and used by other officials and employees in need under the custody of the Municipal Treasurer or GSO designate in compliance with the provisions of Budget Circular No. 2022-1.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented Issued PAR to some concerned personnel to facilitate the cancellation of the motor vehicle. The Management is considering drafting a policy on the borrowing and return of pooled motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the revenue collectors to remit their collections daily or not later than the next banking day, if possible. Otherwise, on a weekly basis or upon reaching the amount of ₱10,000.00 to avoid misuse or loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 4, of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. immediately address the causes of delay or revisit the unimplemented PPAs and review its viability and necessity. In this manner, optimal utilization of the fund could be achieved; and
b. adopt strategies to maximize the utilization of the ensuing 20 percent DF in order to implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to help achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the desired quality of life for its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented Maximize the utilization of the 20% DF. There are still delays in the implementation due to the delay in budget preparation.
observations.reason: There are still delays in the implementation due to the delay in budget preparation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to:
a. implement all programs and projects funded from prior years continuing appropriation, otherwise, if not needed revert to the unexpended portion for re-programming to other activities or projects;
b. prepare and include in the LDRRMFIP the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and
c. perform regular monitoring and evaluation of the implemented PPAs vis-à-vis targets to ensure the attainment of the Municipality’s goals and objectives on disaster response and mitigation.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented Maximize the MDRRM Fund. To include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years in the LDRRMFIP. There is regular monitoring in the implementation of PPAs.
observations.reason: Some projects funded from prior years were not included in QRF and LDRRMFIP. There are still delays in implementation.

observations.managementAction: Not implemented To submit policy regarding hiring of job orders. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 7, of this report.
observations.reason: No policy submitted yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to strictly comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 34 s. 2020 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2021.
observations.managementAction: Not Assigned employees to do the task as required.
observations.reason: No postings on social media platform of the municipality were made by the BAC Sec nor Information Officer.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and Management agreed to stop charging expenses
not related to disaster risk reduction and management from the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to maximize the use of
funds under Section 5 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

2.6. We recommended that Management ensure that all loans granted by the
LGU are covered by a duly signed Loan Agreement, specifying the terms
and conditions, including penalties and consequences in case of delayed
or non-payment. Likewise, conduct periodic follow-ups through written
notices and provide incentives for prompt payment.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the
daily deposit requirement as prescribed by the Manual. If daily deposit is
not practicable, the Municipal Treasurer shall establish and implement a
clear policy on the frequency of deposits and the timely remittance of
collections to mitigate the risk of loss, theft, or mismanagement of
government funds.

4.9. We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Engineer comply with
its duty and function in accordance with Section 477(b)(4) of Republic Act
No. 7160 in providing engineering services to the LGU.

4.10. We also recommended that Management conduct all procurements
through public bidding and resort only to alternative modes if the
circumstances so require in compliance to the requirements of R.A 9184.

5.9. We recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team with a copy
of the contracts entered into by the agency and each of all the documents
forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five working
days from execution thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

6.7. We recommended that Management immediately fill up the vacant
plantilla positions to ensure compliance with the mandated staffing
pattern and human resource management policies.

6.8. We also recommended that Management conduct a review of the
agency’s staffing requirements to determine the necessity of job order
personnel vis-à-vis the available plantilla positions.

7.8. We recommended that Management strictly enforce the deadline for
submitting budget proposals and impose appropriate sanctions on non-
compliant departments to ensure the timely enactment of an ordinance
authorizing the LGU’s annual budget in compliance with the requirements
of the Local Government Code.

8.4. We recommended and the Accounting and Budget Office agreed to
promptly establish a Responsibility Center then assign RC codes or, if
impractical to establish a separate RC, shall maintain subsidiary
ledgers/registries to record all GAD-related expenses and financial
transactions, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008, in order to
facilitate the monitoring, accounting and preparation of the GAD-related
reports.

observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented The Municipal Accountant had adjusted the Inventory account based on documents submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
Fully implemented The Municipal Treasurer is recording the issuances based on the RIS and SSMI.
Fully implemented Physical Count of Inventory for CY 2024 were submitted.
Fully implemented Stock cards are maintained and updated.
Fully implemented The Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer reconciled their balance at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. return to the source agencies all remaining unexpended/unutilized balances of fund transfers or transfer the unexpended balance to the General Fund if the disposition thereof has been specifically provided in the Memorandum of Agreement; and
b. exhaust all efforts to identify the source agency for the unsupported fund transfer emanated prior to CY 2015 totaling amounting to ₱469,672.20, otherwise, a request to the COA Auditor for an authority to write off said dormant account for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented Refunded unutilized balances.
Fully implemented Transferred prior year’s Due to NGA to General Fund on 5/21/2024 ₱469,672.20.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management and Management agreed to direct:
a. the accountable officers to issue and record official receipts in strict numerical sequence pursuant to Section 81.E of the Local Treasury Operations Manual, Book 2.
b. the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the need for another Revenue Collecting Clerk (RCC) to man at the RHU. If warranted, the RCC will be accountable for the use of a different series of ORs to be recorded in a separate cash book.
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented Official receipts are now issued in strict numerical sequence.
Fully implemented A permanent employee was assigned special designation as collection officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to require the BAC Chairman and BAC Members to float RFQs to at least three bona fide suppliers of known qualifications and adhere to the guidelines and procedures of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services using the alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented Purchases were substantiated with at least three RFQs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE continuously implement GAD PPAs as shown in the GPB.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented GAD GPB was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management collect the amount of ₱15,000.00 from a concerned employee who is still connected with the Municipal Government of Lubuagan in order to fully settle his outstanding balance.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented The employee submitted accomplishment / liquidation in relation to the ₱15,000.00 outstanding account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the municipal mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan exercise their prerogative to revise and update their Revenue Code in accordance with Sections 129 and 191 of the Local Government Code to meet the financial needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented The Updated Revenue Code was release in January 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management conduct immediate and proper disposition of unserviceable and other properties no longer needed to avoid further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented Unserviceable properties were already disposed/ sold on 7/25/2024 for ₱35,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for in-kind donations, Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations and Reports on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of In-Kind Donations, and furnish the same to Audit Team within ten working days after the end of the quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: Management assured that for CY 2022, Quarterly Report be submitted to the audit team as required under COA Circular 2020-009.
observations.reason: No report on donations was submitted for CY 2021 however review of journals revealed that there were in kind donations received from the Department of Health.

a. The OIC Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the preparation of the road map of the municipality in order to facilitate the inventory taking and reporting of the road network system; and
b. The Municipal Engineer provides the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road projects.

observations.recommendation:
1.19 We recommended that:

a. the Municipal Accountant effect the proposed adjusting entries to
correct balances of affected accounts and, henceforth, ensure that
all acquired properties are booked up and that all
donated/transferred/disposed properties are duly derecognized in
the books;

b. the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer/Inventory
Committee regularly reconcile their records to ensure that
discrepancies, if any, are promptly corrected and balances of
accounts in the financial statements are duly supported;

c. Management conduct immediate and proper disposition of
unserviceable and other properties no longer needed to avoid
further deterioration; and

d. Management comply with COA Circular 80-124 dated June 18,
1980 and COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 on the
provision of Property Number and other vital information on all
government properties in order to prevent possible misuse and/or
loss of government property.

2.13 We recommended and Management agreed to always follow the
provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement/Understanding they
entered into and adhere with pertinent guidelines of DBM-DOH JC
Nos. 1 and 2 s. 2020 in the payment of AHDP and SRA.

3.7 We recommended that management revert to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund the unutilized Special Trust Fund aged
more than 5 years which shall be made available for other social services
after enactment by the local sanggunian.

3.8 We also recommended that the agency adjust the Trust Liability
Account to fairly state the balances of liability and government equity
and henceforth ensure that the unimplemented infrastructure projects
funded under the DRRM funds classified as Capital Outlay shall not be
transferred to the Special Trust Fund account.

4.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in
compliance to COA-Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in
order to monitor the usage of the LDRRM Funds and DRRM funds
from national government agencies and other local government units.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the
provisions of Administrative Order No. 34 s. 2020 and GPPB Circular
No. 01-2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant properly coordinate to determine the basis of receivable balances presented in the Financial Statements supported with figures in terms of property owners and/or title numbers.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our previous recommendations that:
a. the Municipal Treasurer, acting as the GSO, prepare the Report on Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) based on the summary of the RIS on a monthly or quarterly basis as deemed applicable;
b. the Municipal Accountant record the purchase of supplies and materials as inventories even if these are immediately issued to end-users since these are still carried in stock at their respective offices and subsequently record the issuance/consumption of inventories as expense by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) supported by the RSMI from the GSO; and
c. for subsequent transactions, use the RIS for all requisition of supplies as basis of the GSO in reporting its utilization.

We recommended that Management:
a. create an Inventory Committee to be responsible for the conduct of annual physical inventory of all municipal properties and the preparation of the prescribed Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) upon completion of inventory;
b. require the Municipal Treasurer as the General Service Officer designate to maintain and update property cards for all properties and regularly renew their corresponding Property Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (PARE).
c. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and regularly update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and other subsidiary ledgers to include the acquisition dates of PPEs needed in the preparation of the lapsing schedule of PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
• The Management have created Inventory Executive Order No. Committee to monitor and Reorganizing the conduct physical Inventory, Appraisal properties. Committee was issued in September 7, 2020.
• The Accounting and Treasury Office have updated their records of maintaining updated copies of Property Cards and PAREs are now renewed.
• PPE Ledger Cards are now maintained at the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. effect the following entry to correct the misclassified accounts;
b. observe proper accounting treatment and use only appropriate accounts to fairly present balances of the financial statements in succeeding transactions; and
c. evaluate the propriety of recorded accounts payable taking into consideration the existence of supporting document and/or proofs of the actual delivery of goods and services, otherwise, effect necessary adjustments in the books to conform with regulations.
observations.managementAction:
• The municipal accountant corrected the misclassified entries through an adjusting entry as recommended.
• Proper recording of payable accounts are now being observed.
• Adjustments are now being effected and subsequent stale checks are now properly accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a periodic reconciliation of cash accounts and submit to the audit team bank reconciliation statements in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular 96-11 and likewise, effect adjustment of the stale checks still outstanding in the books.
observations.managementAction: Periodic bank reconciliation statements are already being prepared. Adjustments are now effected and subsequent stale checks are now properly accounted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the following offices concerned to adhere with the required procedures in order to ensure effective monitoring and recording of the inventory items of the LGU:
a. Municipal Accounting Office:
• record purchases as inventory upon receipt of deliveries and recognize them as expense upon receipt of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) or distribution list as may be appropriate, from the office concerned; and
• maintain a Supplies Ledger Card for each class of inventory for easy reconciliation with the records of the treasury office.
b. Municipal Treasury Office
• record receipt of supplies in the supply stock card for each class of inventory;
• release and record issuance of supplies to end users upon receipt of the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS); and
• consolidate the RIS weekly using the SSMI and submit the same to the accounting Office as their basis in recording of supplies expenses.
c. Other concerned departments:
• submit copy of liquidation/distribution report to the accounting office upon completion of the activity to serve as basis in recording the expenses.
observations.managementAction:
• The SSMI shall become the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording expenses. However, concerned departments are still required to prepare and maintain copies of liquidation/distribution reports.
• The Accounting Office has maintained supplies ledger card for each class of inventory and being updated regularly.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.2, Part II of this report. Several tasks under the recommendation are still not implemented by the Municipal Treasury Office and other offices (recording receipts in stock card, release and record issuance upon RIS, consolidate RIS weekly) as indicated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the BAC Secretariat strictly abide with the rules set by Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised IRR pertaining to the procurement process in order to ensure transparency and competitiveness in procurement transactions.

observations.managementAction: The 2020 PPMP already reflects the prices based on the 2019 DPRI. Compliance to the DPRI in CY 2020 procurements of Drugs and Medicines are now observed.

We  recommended that Management:
create an Inventory Committee to be responsible for the conduct of annual physical inventory of all municipal properties and the preparation of the prescribed Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) upon completion of inventory;
require the Municipal Treasurer  as the General Service Officer designate to maintain and update property cards for all PPEs and regularly renew their corresponding (Property Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (PARE).
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and regularly update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and other subsidiary ledgers to include the acquisition dates of PPEs needed in the preparation of the lapsing schedule of PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
effect the  following entry to correct the misclassified accounts:
observe  proper accounting treatment and use only appropriate accounts to fairly present balances of the financial statements in succeeding transactions; and
evaluate the propriety of recorded accounts payable taking into consideration the existence of supporting document and/or proofs of the actual delivery of goods and services, otherwise, effect necessary adjustments in the books to conform with regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the details of the Road Networks account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
The Municipal Accountant commented that the details of road networks account were not properly disclosed in the notes to financial statements due to the absence of road map and road networks inventory. However, the LGU is in the process of updating their road network system. Hence, they agreed to disclose in it the notes as soon as they complete their inventory and committed to make the necessary adjustment in the books, if any.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a periodic reconciliation of cash accounts and submit to the audit team bank reconciliation statements in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular 96-11 and likewise, effect adjustment of the stale checks  still outstanding in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (a) prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all recording items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and (b) perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the following offices concerned to adhere with the  required procedures in order to ensure effective monitoring and recording  of the inventory items of the LGU:
Municipal Accounting Office:
record purchases as inventory upon receipt of deliveries and recognize them as expense upon receipt of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) or distribution list as may be appropriate, from the office concerned; and
maintain a Supplies Ledger Card for each class of inventory for easy reconciliation with the records of the treasury office.
Municipal Treasury Office
record receipt of  supplies in the supply stock card for each class of inventory;
release and record issuance of supplies to end users upon receipt of the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS); and
consolidate the RIS weekly using the SSMI  and submit the same to the accounting Office as their basis in recording  of supplies expenses.
Other concerned departments:
submit copy of liquidation/distribution report to the accounting office upon completion of the activity to serve as basis in recording expenses.

We recommended that the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the BAC Secretariat strictly abide with the rules set by Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised IRR pertaining to the procurement process in order to ensure transparency and competitiveness in procurement transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Office of the Rural Health Units to revise their existing Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to conform with the latest Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) prescribed by the DOH.

We recommended that Management furnish the Auditor copy of contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection and all documents forming part thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-01.
Management Comment
Management agreed to our audit recommendation and assured the audit to submit contracts within 5 days upon its perfection.
We have recommended that the Municipal accountant treat all the excess amount of the unutilized collections from sale of bid documents under the general fund books of the LGU.
observations.reason: The amount has already been reverted to the General Fund in May 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the MESWMB coordinate with the barangays relative to their plans and programs particularly on the immediate construction of MRF.
observations.reason: Construction of MRF was completed in November 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management cause the immediate sale of the unserviceable properties to prevent their reduction in value and loss of additional income that could accrue to the government.
observations.managementAction: Partial sale was done for the unserviceable properties and planned to dispose the other properties through negotiated sale since the sole bidder backed out after bidding process.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections promptly and intact in order to prevent misappropriation, theft or loss of funds.
observations.reason: The amount has already been deposited and subsequent collections deposited promptly and intact.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the audit team with the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year.
We recommended that management implement ADM projects within the year these were programmed to allow timely delivery of basic services and facilities of constituents of the municipality.
observations.reason: All of the three (3) ADM projects were awarded/implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the DRRM Office to closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects/programs and activities funded out of LDRRMF to institutionalize measures to reduce disaster risk and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the agency has an overall utilization rate of 92% for the Mitigation and preparedness program of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause/demand the immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason: The amount of      ₱ 9,751,276.76 remained unliquidated as at December 31, 2019.  The amount of            ₱ 8,383, 872.69 pertains to the accountability of a former mayor whose case is still pending in court. The balance of      ₱ 1,367,404.07 pertains to unliquidated cash advances of AOs. no. longer connected with the LGU who were likewise previously issued demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

   require the DRRM Office to closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects/programs and activities funded out of LDRRMF to institutionalize measures to reduce disaster risk and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels; and

   require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRM Investment Plan indicating the list of priority projects and activities for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for prior years so the amount will be used efficiently and effectively for the intended purpose.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, out of the P7,655,088.91 appropriated for LDDRM Fund, only P4,049,697.85 or 52.9% was utilized, leaving a balance of P3,605,391.06 at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, through the Local Planning and Development Council submit GAD Accomplishment Report and GAD Data base that were used as their basis in the attribution of GAD plan and budget not later than end of January 31 of the ensuing year to have basis in assessing if the LGU’s GAD budget is gender responsive and aligned with the National government’s commitment on ensuring gender responsive governance.
observations.managementAction: GAD accomplishment report was not yet completed due to difficulty in determining the actual cost of the programmed activities as embodied in the GAD plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause/ demand the immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to the accountable officers who have outstanding cash advances as at year end.
observations.reason: Out of the P9,751,276.76, P8,383,872.69 pertained to the outstanding cash advance balance of the former mayor subject of a case still pending at the Ombudsman. The remaining balance of P1,367,404.07 pertained to outstanding cash advances of AOs who were no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Accounting office and the Treasury Office to maintain the PPELC and Property Cards, respectively, for each category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile records and reports periodically.
observations.managementAction: Records were maintained but no reconciliation was made between the Accounting office and the Treasury Office.
observations.reason: As at year end, no PPE inventory report was submitted thereby precluding the audit team from verifying accuracy of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt a strategies for the timely implementation of the 20% EDF to ensure the maximum utilization and attainment of desired objectives. Further, we recommended that programmed projects prior to CY 2015 be reviewed to assess the necessity of said projects, otherwise, reprogram the budgeted amount to other priority projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer asserted that the reason for low implementation was the late approval of the budget and his office was undermanned, thus preventing them from implementing the current year’s PPAs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand for the immediate return of the motor vehicle issued to the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: No management action taken by the management.
observations.reason: Management should exert extra effort to retrieve the vehicle issued to the former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
revert all the excess balances of the completed projects sourced from the local government fund as counterpart to the project for re-appropriation in succeeding year; and
remit to the source agency all the excess amounts of fund transfers received.

We have recommended that:
the OIC- Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the preparation of the road map of the Municipality in order to facilitate the inventory taking and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; and
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road projects.
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to treat all the excess amount of the unutilized collections from sale of bid documents as income under the General Fund books of the LGU.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the undeposited collections of former Treasurers to their proper accounts inorder that the financial statements of the LGU will be fairly presented.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant already reclassified the undeposited collections of former Treasurers to its proper account.

Adjustment was made in January 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
trace all CIP transactions recorded in prior years and reclassify completed projects to the proper account in consonance with the PPSAS to avoid misleading information; and

strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 -Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units particularly in the reclassification of Construction in Progress accounts to their corresponding asset accounts upon completion of the project.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant already reclassified the completed projects to its proper accounts.

Adjustment was made in March 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

 the Municipal Accountant record Office supplies, Accountable forms, Agricultural and Marine, drugs, medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies as Inventory upon receipt of delivery and recognize as Expense only upon actual use or disposition based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued; and 

b.   the Municipal Administrator instruct concerned officials to submit regularly the report of inventory of supplies  as well as issuances,  distributions or sale of inventory, to the Office the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording and adjustment in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the supplies as inventory upon receipt of delivery as recommended.
observations.reason: Supplies were recorded as inventory upon receipt of delivery, however, review made on the entries showed that the inventories were still carried in the books although the same had already been used and distributed as at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed:

to adjust the recorded AP to the proper accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of accounts for Local Government Units; and

for succeeding transactions, strictly follow the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs for recording transactions in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant complied with the audit recommendations.

Adjustment was made in March 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that certified true copy of the Job Order Contract/ Contract of Service of personnel hired on a contractual basis or piece meal basis with statement of the expected work or output be attached to comply with the documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 pertaining to the payment of wages of employees on job orders.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the audit recommendation

The HRMO had prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor Job Order Contract/ Contract of Service of personnel hired on a contractual basis with statement of the expected work or output.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management implement ADM projects within the year these were programmed to allow timely delivery of basic services and facilities to constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: An agreement was made between the LGU and DPWH wherein the latter will implement the drilling and elevated water tank while the LGU is in charged with the pipelines to be connected to the water tank. However, the project of the DPWH was terminated thereby rendering the LGU project amounting to P4,000,000.00 unimplemented.
observations.reason:
Only two projects totaling P4,873,000.00 were completed as at year end. 
We recommend that the LGU should make representation to the DPWH to pursue the implementation of the project as agreed upon in order to implement the project of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management exert effort to efficiently and effectively implement pro-poor programs in order to attain its goals and objectives, thus, uplifting the life of the intended beneficiaries on a timely manner.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board prepare and formulate the yearly plans and programs to support the activities in the attainment of its targets as laid down in the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and include in the yearly plans and programs the formal establishment and frame of reference of MRF in every barangay, segregation at source, segregated collection, and other activities that will ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board chaired by the Municipal Mayor prepare annual SEF Budget to supplement fund for other expenditures of public schools in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and/or update the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts of the LGU, effect the adjustments accordingly on all reconciling items, and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction:
The management complied with the audit recommendation.

Adjustment was made in December 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible official of offices concerned to submit required reports in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGU's, Volume I and II and COA Circular Nos. 96-011 and 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of two accounts and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted. Henceforth, prepare the monthly reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and ensure that all reconciling items are resolved or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review of transactions to minimize errors and/or omissions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: A difference of Ᵽ36,794.99 between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts was not yet reconciled due to undetermined beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strategies for the timely implementation of the 20% EDF to ensure the maximum utilization and attainment of desired objectives. Further, we recommended that programmed projects prior to CY 2015 be reviewed to assess the necessity of said projects, otherwise, reprogram the budgeted amount to other priority projects.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU has implemented 24 projects out of the 28 projects for CY 2016. Constant monitoring of projects was also done by the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer explained that the delay in the implementation of the 20% DF for CY 2017 was due to  lack of technical staff at the MEO to handle voluminous projects under Trust Funds which requires  immediate implementation. Nevertheless, they informed the team that  the 2017 projects under  20% DF are now implemented as of March, 2018.
observations.reason:
It was noted that only the CY 2016 projects were implemented in CY 2017, however, targeted projects for CY 2017 and prior to 2016 were not implemented. 

We recommend further that un-implemented projects prior to 2016 should be reassessed if it’s still necessary for the development of the LGU, otherwise, consider reprogramming to other needs of the community.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
Management regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan to ensure the optimum implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs;  and

the Local Chief Executive consider the appointment of a full time LDRRMO responsible in the efficient implementation or management of disaster risk reduction and mitigation programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor appointed LDRRMO to monitor implementation of disaster risk reduction and mitigation programs and activities in the person of Mr. Romel Salbang
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD focal person to submit promptly the GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding audit year as stated in Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated May 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the GAD Accomplishment report for 2017 on April 5, 2018.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive demand for the immediate return of the motor vehicle issued to the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: We were informed that demand cannot be issued since the accountable officer whom the vehicle was issued is still a fugitive as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Management should exert more efforts to retrieve the vehicle. We informed them to coordinate with the LTO, if possible, to check its location if it is still in use by the former Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the refund of the overpayment of salaries.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management since the case was appealed at the COA Central Office filed by the aggrieved party, (Bookkeeper). They agreed to deduct from her terminal leave upon receipt of the decision of her appeal.
observations.reason: Awaiting decision of the COA Central Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and/or update the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts of the LGU, effect the adjustments accordingly on all reconciling items, and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office prepared all the Bank Reconciliation Statements on the accounts of the LGU.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted but the reconciling items were not yet taken up in the books.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made some adjustments in the books to correct the affected accounts.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented.

Adjustments were recorded in the books in January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause/demand the immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances and ensure strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Enforced full liquidation of the outstanding cash advances to all officers and employees with overdue cash advances.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, the unliquidated cash advances decreased by P6,184,205.80 due to the refund made by some officials and the former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office to maintain the PPELC and Property Cards, respectively, for each category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile records and reports periodically.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office already maintained property cards for each category of PPE.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury Office already maintained property cards for each category of PPE, however, no reconciliation was done with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign a person to monitor and device a feedback or reporting mechanism on the progress of funded projects to be implemented in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Management designated Mr. Charles Osdeg to monitor and make reports and updates management regarding the progress of funded projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting department start establishing the Special Accounts in the General Fund and its corresponding subsidiary ledger to conform with the requirements provided for under the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: Maintained subsidiary ledger to monitor utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Material Recovery Facility (MRF) be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays which may initially be piloted in one barangay in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/ participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.
observations.managementAction: Awichon, Upper Uma, Lubuagan, was considered as the pilot barangay for the MRF. For CY 2018, a biodegradable shredder from DENR-EMB-CAR shall be installed.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, the LGU budgeted for the construction of MRF in every barangay of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the engineering personnel and BAC members to ensure that the required documents are complete and properly evaluated prior to the prosecution of project; and the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of documents before processing payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: Management was vigilant in processing  claims/transactions such that the documentary requirements are complete before payment made.
observations.reason: Based on post-audit, the required supporting documents were properly attached to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible officials of offices concerned to submit required reports in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGU’s, Volume I and II and COA Circular Nos. 96-011 and 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Submission of reports not beyond the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted 3 to 4 months after the prescribed period of submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a GAD M&E Team to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: GAD M&E Team was already created to evaluate outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to Immediately deposit all cash collections remaining in her custody, restitute her  cash shortage, and henceforth observe the frequency of deposits required under the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I; and
observations.managementAction: Required the OIC-Mun. Treasurer to immediately deposit all cash collections in her custody and to observe depositing of collections weekly.
observations.reason: The amount of              P590,707.46 was deposited on February 2, 2018 leaving a balance of                      P1,177,593.48 which was recommended to be reclassified to Other Receivables account because this is a cash accountability of the former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted. Henceforth, prepare monthly reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and ensure that all reconciling items are resolved or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review of transactions to minimize errors and/or omissions.
observations.managementAction: Partial reconciliation was made but due to volume of transactions in the Accounting Department, however, they committed to complete their reconciliation before the end of CY 2018.
observations.reason: The discrepancy of P2,474,396.47 decreased by 85% leaving unreconciled balance of Ᵽ356,126.67  as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management ensure that at least 5 percent of its total appropriation for the General Fund is set aside for the implementation of GAD related projects and activities to address the various gender issues in the Municipality as required in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA. Likewise, an overall GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared to support the allocation of the required amount of GAD budget for the year.

We likewise recommended that the LGU facilitate the creation of the LGU GAD focal Point System who shall be tasked to prepare and submit the required Annual GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated at least 5% of its total appropriation for the implementation of GAD related projects. Likewise, created the LGU’s GAD Focal Point System.
observations.reason:
For CY 2017, P3,783,884.15 was budgeted for GAD equivalent to 5% of the P75,677,683.00 total appropriation.





Fully Implemented.

EO No. 2017-31 dated May 30, 2017 was issued by the Municipal Mayor for the creation of GFPS.

observations.recommendation:
In order to strengthen controls over cash and correct the deficiencies noted in audit, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to:

Immediately deposit all cash collections remaining in her custody, restitute her  cash shortage, and henceforth observe the frequency of deposits required under the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I; and

Use separate sets of accountable forms for GF, TF and SEF and instruct RCCs to issue them chronologically.

Direct the OIC, Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of granting IOU’s to officers and employees.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted. Henceforth, prepare monthly reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and ensure that all reconciling items are resolved or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review of transactions to minimize errors and/or omissions.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was on-going, but due to volume of transactions in the Accounting Department, personnel involved could not maximize their effort to attain its realization on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Engineering Office to submit to COA the required list of on-going and completed projects and monthly monitoring report within the prescribed period; and

Ensure strict compliance of the public’s right to information on awarded and on-going projects by posting of signboards for infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs, regardless of the amount and sources of funds, complete with the required information and in the conspicuous places within the agency premises or at the PPA location/ venue to inform public of the essential features/ elements of the PPAs.
observations.managementAction: No action made by the management.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the General Service Officer to initiate the process of converting the private plates into government plates those motor vehicles that the LGU will not dispose of.


Being the one who is immediately and primarily responsible for all government funds and property pertaining to his agency should demand for the immediate return of the motor vehicle issued to the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction:
The management conducted inventory, and inspection of the said green plate vehicles showed that they are not serviceable.


The management informed that they could hardly demand the return of the vehicle issued and taken by the former treasurer because the latter is at large.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly with regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated  February 10, 1997.
We recommended that the LGU management direct the refund of the overpayment of salaries.
observations.managementAction: Case appealed at the COA Central Office filed by  the aggrieved party, (Bookkeeper)
observations.reason: Awaiting decision of the COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and/or update
the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository  accounts  of
the LGU, effect the adjustments  accordingly  on  all  reconciling
items, and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


a) install control measures to see to it that journal entries  are
   properly taken up in the books in accordance with GAAP;


b) effect the following proposed adjusting entries to correct  the
   balance of the affected accounts.


|Account Title             | Dr         | Cr       |
|Due from officers and     |  375,249.50|          |
|Employees                 |            |          |
|Other Receivable          |  213,848.03|          |
|Prior Period Adjustments  |            |          |
|                          |            |589,097.53|

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  cause/demand  the
immediate liquidation of overdue cash advances and  ensure  strict
adherence to the provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  97-002  dated
February 10, 1997.

We recommended that management require the Accounting  Office  and
the Treasury Office to maintain  the  PPELC  and  Property  Cards,
respectively, for each category of property, plant  and  equipment
and to reconcile records and reports periodically.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign a  person  to
monitor and device  a  feedback  or  reporting  mechanism  on  the
progress of funded projects to be implemented in  accordance  with
the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strategies for
the timely implementation of the 20% EDF  to  ensure  the  maximum
utilization and attainment  of  desired  objectives.  Further,  we
recommended that programmed projects prior to CY 2015 be  reviewed
to assess the necessity of said projects, otherwise, reprogram the
budgeted amount to other priority projects.

We recommended that the Accounting department  start  establishing
the Special Accounts in the General  Fund  and  its  corresponding
subsidiary ledger to conform with the  requirements  provided  for
under the New Government Accounting System  for  Local  Government
Units.

We recommended that:


a) Management regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization  of
   LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities  listed
   in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development  plans
   and annual work and financial  plan  to  ensure   the  optimum
   implementation of  the  pre-disaster  preparedness  programs;
   and


b) the Local Chief Executive consider the appointment of a  full
   time LDRRMO responsible in the  efficient  implementation  or
   management of disaster risk reduction and mitigation programs
   and activities.

We recommended that Material Recovery Facility be established in
every barangay or cluster of  barangays  which  may  initially  be
piloted in one barangay in  order  to  institute  a  system  of
knowledge in establishing one  as  well  as  to  gain  commitment/
participation of barangay officials on the proper administration
and the operation of MRF.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  require  the
engineering personnel and BAC members to ensure that the  required
documents  are  complete  and  properly  evaluated  prior  to  the
prosecution of project; and the Municipal  Accountant  ensure  the
completeness of documents before processing payment of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  direct  the  responsible
officials of offices  concerned  to  submit  required  reports  in
accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGU’s, Volume I and II and COA
Circular Nos. 96-011 and 2013-004.

We recommended that Management create a GAD M&E Team to  evaluate  the
outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that Management ensure strict compliance to the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances by adopting the following measures:

Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of records from time to time to prevent and detect any errors that might occur and to effect immediate correction thereof. Also, require them to indicate references in both the cash book and accounting records including liquidation reports to properly match cash advances and liquidations.
 
Cause the immediate liquidation/ refund of overdue cash advances by concerned accountable officers in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 so that the affected accounts will be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Cash book and accounting records of the Municipal Accountant and the newly designated OIC- Municipal Treasurer have been reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management remit immediately to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized/ remaining balance of PDAF for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the National Government, in compliance with SC Decision.
We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking documents to support the payments of honoraria pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001, otherwise Notice of Suspension shall be issued.

We recommended that Management require the GAD focal person to submit promptly the GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding audit year as stated in Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated May 18, 2014.

We have recommended that Management require the PCL President to refund the amount of ₱48,468.75 representing illegal payment of Transportation Allowance claimed for the period January to December 2014 and, henceforth, stop granting Transportation Allowance to government officials with assigned government vehicle in compliance to the provisions of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The balance of P36,093.75 was already refunded in December 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management ensure that at least 5 percent of its total appropriation for the General Fund is set aside for the implementation of GAD related projects and activities to address the various gender issues in the Municipality as required in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA. Likewise, an overall GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared to support the allocation of the required amount of GAD budget for the year.

We likewise recommended that the LGU facilitate the creation of the LGU GAD focal Point System who shall be tasked to prepare and submit the required Annual GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The management asserted that GAD budget were incorporated in every department, when totaled it exceeds the 5% GAD budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that rules and regulations on the preparation of the Program of Works be complied with to avoid similar disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer agreed to pay the disallowance amounting to ₱65,272.14.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management undertake Physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality and a report thereon be submitted in accordance with the regulation to determine their existence, validity and accuracy of the account balances.
observations.managementAction: Report on Inventory of PPE was submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
